regimeSub: Brent breaches $100 on Hormuz fighting resumption; VIX creeps to 18.7 as gold slips from ATH.
themes: Brent crude +$4.27 to $100.17 as OPEC+ Aug quota hike (Jul 6) collides with resumed Middle East fighting (Jul 13 IER) → geopolitical premium now dominant over supply expansion; WTI $91.64 confirms $90+ floor

KOSPI -2.1% to 6816 extending Kimi K3 selloff (Jul 20) despite Alphabet AI capex boost (Jul 23) and TSMC $100bn US investment (Jul 16) → BOK 2.75% hike (Jul 16) fails to support KRW as USDKRW firms to 1464

10y yield +4bp to 4.67% with 2s10s at 36bp as Williams 'inflation peaked' (Jul 15) clashes with crude surge → CPI 3.5% YoY holding while energy pressures rebuild; Warsh FOMC split (Jul 8 mins) keeps hike optionality alive
Gold -$72 to $4047 off ATH as VIX rises to 18.7 from 16.64 → risk repricing underway with Trump Iran sanctions push (Jul 19) keeping tail risk bid but profit-taking on haven metals into crude spike

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10y real ~1.17% (4.67 nom - 3.5 CPI); positive real rate constrains but energy shock complicates Fed math

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 36bp steepness (4.31/4.67) reflects term premium rebuild on inflation persistence; not inverted = no imminent recession

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads stable as energy sector benefits from crude surge; watch for cracks if Brent sustains above $100

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.7 up from 16.64; vol normalizing as energy squeeze and geopolitics warrant modest hedging increase

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $100.17 breaches triple digits on Hormuz resumption (Jul 13); OPEC+ quota hike irrelevant vs geopolitics

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4047 down $72 from ATH; profit-taking as crude absorbs geopolitical bid; still elevated on Iran risk

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1464 firming despite BOK 2.75% hike (Jul 16); AI chip demand concerns outweigh rate support

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: TSMC capex boost (Jul 16) and Alphabet spend (Jul 23) positive but Kimi K3 shock (Jul 20) clouds Korea names

headline: Brent crude breaches $100 on resumed Hormuz hostilities, lifting VIX to 18.7 while KOSPI extends AI selloff and gold retreats from ATH amid Fed data-dependency under Warsh.
